rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scope hierarchy when inserting annex blocks
Ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the target scope
Preserve existing architecture by not modifying component connections or subcomponents during insertion
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax for AGREE contracts
Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when bounds are concrete
Relate outputs to inputs in component type dependency guarantees
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
